title: Identify a Safer Walking Route
summary: Choose a walking route that is well-lit, populated, and safe.
learnerPromise: You will be able to look at walking options and pick a route that feels safer and more comfortable.
whyItMatters: Choosing a safer route can reduce risk and help you feel more confident when walking, especially at night or in unfamiliar areas.
realLifeUses: Picking a route home from school or work that has more people and lights
Avoiding poorly lit streets or isolated shortcuts at night
Finding a path with sidewalks or crosswalks instead of busy roads
Helping a friend choose a safe walking route in a new neighborhood
materialsNeeded: A map app or paper map of the area
Optional: a trusted person to discuss the route with
result: A chosen walking route that you feel reasonably safe using.
youWillLearn: How to spot well-lit and populated streets on a map
How to avoid isolated or poorly maintained paths
How to check for sidewalks, crosswalks, and traffic patterns
How to adjust your route based on time of day
miniChallenge: Look at two possible routes to a place you go often. Pick the one that feels safer and walk it once, noting what you liked or did not like.
steps: Open a map or think about two possible routes to your destination.
Look for well-lit streets, sidewalks, and areas with other people.
Avoid shortcuts through isolated alleys, parks, or unlit areas.
Consider the time of day and how busy the streets usually are.
Walk the route and notice how comfortable and safe you feel.
completionCriteria: You compared at least two possible walking routes.
You chose the one with better lighting, people, or sidewalks.
You walked the route and felt reasonably safe.
commonProblems: Choosing the shortest route without thinking about safety
Walking through unfamiliar areas without checking them first
Forgetting that a safe daytime route may not be safe at night
tips: Walk new routes during daylight first to learn the area.
Trust your feelings. If a street feels uncomfortable, choose another.
Tell someone which route you are taking if walking alone.
Street lights, open shops, and other walkers are good signs of a safer route.
helpBoundary: Ask a trusted adult, police officer, or transit worker for help if you feel unsafe on a route or if you are unsure which way to go in an unfamiliar area.
